subject: SCIENCE
title: Living or Nonliving?
objective: Use multiple characteristics as evidence when discussing living things.
introduction: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Living or Nonliving?”.
A key word used in the lesson “Living or Nonliving?”.
A key word used in the lesson “Living or Nonliving?”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.

examples: seed can be dormant and living
fire moves but is not an organism

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

fits: seed can be dormant and living
fire moves but is not an organism

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

options: Movement alone does not prove something is living.

feedback.correct: Correct: Movement alone does not prove something is living.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. Everything that moves is alive. and Dormant seeds are always dead. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

pairs.left: seed can be dormant and living
fire moves but is not an organism

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Living or Nonliving?”?

options.text: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

options.text: seed can be dormant and living

items.explanation: The correct response is “seed can be dormant and living” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: seed can be dormant and living
fire moves but is not an organism

items.explanation: The correct response is “Movement alone proves life” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

options.text: Movement alone does not prove something is living.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Movement alone does not prove something is living.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “plant grows and uses energy” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living organisms use energy, maintain internal processes, grow or develop, respond, and belong to reproducing life cycles; one visible sign alone is not enough.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Living or Nonliving?; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Living or Nonliving?.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
